metadata.description: 版本控制: Version control records the history of file changes, supporting collaborative development and rollbacks. Git is currently the most popular version control system. Python code example (to illustrate a basic concept in Git): ```python import subprocess def git_clone(repo_url, destination_path): try: # Clone a repository using Git command result = subprocess.run(['git', 'clone', repo_url, destination_path], check=True) print("Repository cloned successfully to:", destination_path) except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e: print(f"Failed to clone the repository: {e}") # Example usage repo_url = "https://github.com/user/repo.git" destination_path = "./my_repo" git_clone(repo_url, destination_path) ``` This Python function `git_clone` demonstrates how you might use a subprocess to run a Git command for cloning a repository. Note that this is a simplified example and does not cover all aspects of version control or Git usage.
